title: image_descriptor: caption the current image into the prompt box (dev radial) — text-only workflow needs a completion path
description: Wire the `image_descriptor` workflow (already authored, lives at comfy_workflows/raw/image_descriptor.json) into the app behind a DEV radial-menu action: read the current image, inject its path into Input_Image, run, and write the returned caption into the prompt box. PURPOSE: creative upscalers and Apps need a FAITHFUL description of the actual pixels — users re-use their thin original prompt on upscale and lose the detail the model hallucinated, giving mediocre results. Re-captioning the RESULT fixes it. Dev-gated on purpose: this is an in-app test harness to judge whether caption->upscale actually improves output before committing to a shipped UI (the product surface would be a button on the prompt box next to the positive/negative toggle — NOT part of this card). Uses Qwen3-VL 4B via ComfyUI's native `Generate Text` node — the SAME qwen3vl_4b_fp8_scaled text encoder Krea2 already loads, so zero extra download and zero new dependency. KNOWN BLOCKER, verified in source: generationService.js:769 treats a zero-media completion as a CANCELLED generation and discards the caption. Root fix needed — see brief.md, do NOT guard-clause it.

attention.reason: Dev harness SHIPPED and engine-verified (15.1s dense caption). NEW BLOCKER, user-reproduced: Qwen3-VL 4B REFUSES on some images ("I am sorry, but I can not fulfill that request") - the brief's "NSFW handled accurately" rested on ONE image and is now falsified. use_default_template=false does NOT change it, so refusal is weights-deep not template-driven. Testing Huihui-Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct-abliterated fp8_scaled (5.24GB, same size/quant as the current encoder = drop-in clip_name swap). NOTE: the card's creation event lists abliterated variants as rejected - that rejection assumed stock handled NSFW and is now VOID. The text-only completion contract (generationService zero-media = cancelled) remains open and untouched; the harness bypasses it via runImageDescribe.
